title: rosa dumalis subsp. squarrosula
intro: Rosa dumalis subsp. squarrosula is a lesser-known subspecies of Rosa dumalis, a species of wild rose native to mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rosa dumalis subsp. squarrosula include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
